From: Dave Hawkins <iamdavehawkins@gmail.com>
Sent: Monday, August 25, 2025 2:17 PM
To: Johnston, Mike - MO Mayor <Mike.Johnston@denvergov.org>; Ford, Amy - DOTI HE3258 Manager Department of Transportation and Infrastructure <Amy.Ford@denvergov.org>; Cieciek, Gregory M. - DOTI Project Manager Engineering Senior <Gregory.Cieciek@denvergov.org>; Lanphier, Molly - DOTI Project Manager Engineering Senior <Molly.Lanphier@denvergov.org>
Cc: Alvidrez, Flor C. - CC Member District 7 Denver City Council <Flor.Alvidrez@denvergov.org>
Subject: [EXTERNAL] 182 signatures - Community Letter of Support for the Alameda Lanes Repurposing Project
Dear Mayor Johnston, Ms. Ford, and DOTI Representatives:

Preface: This letter represents an organic grassroots effort organized by David Hawkins, a local father concerned about traffic safety in our neighborhood, neighbors, and community members. The signatures were collected through our community website safer-alameda.com<http:/safer-alameda.com>, where neighbors could learn about the project and add their support. Over several weeks we have gathered 182 signatures from community members who support this important safety initiative.

We, the undersigned residents, business owners, and community members, write to express our strong support for the Alameda Lanes Repurposing Project. This initiative represents thoughtful, evidence-based transportation planning that will improve safety, enhance livability, and benefit our community for years to come.

Why We Support This Project

Enhanced Safety for All Users: The project's safety improvements including protected crossings, pedestrian islands, and reduced crossing distances will significantly improve safety for pedestrians, cyclists, and drivers alike. These evidence-based design elements have proven successful in cities across the nation.

Improved Traffic Flow: Dedicated turn lanes will prevent turning vehicles from blocking through traffic, actually improving traffic flow and reducing congestion. This is a win-win solution that addresses mobility while prioritizing safety.

Neighborhood Protection: Strategic bollards and turn restrictions will prevent dangerous cut-through traffic in residential areas, making our neighborhoods safer for families and children. This protects the residential character of our community.

Economic Benefits: Research consistently shows that walkable streets with appropriate traffic calming measures increase local business revenue. Slower, safer streets encourage people to stop, shop, and engage with local businesses.

Environmental Responsibility: By creating more walkable infrastructure and encouraging sustainable transportation options, this project aligns with Denver's climate goals and contributes to improved air quality for our community.

Addressing Concerns with Facts

We understand that some community members have expressed concerns about this project. However, extensive research and real-world examples demonstrate that well-designed road reconfigurations like this one:

Typically have minimal impact on travel times while dramatically improving safety
Reduce serious crashes by up to 25% according to Federal Highway Administration studies

Improve pedestrian safety by up to 40% with protected crossing infrastructure
Support local economic development through increased walkability Maintain emergency vehicle access while improving response times through reduced congestion

Our Request

We urge DOTI to move forward with the Alameda Lanes Repurposing Project as planned. This project represents the kind of forward-thinking, evidence-based transportation planning that Denver needs to create safer, more livable neighborhoods for all residents.

The safety of our community members—pedestrians, cyclists, and drivers —should be our top priority. This project achieves that goal while maintaining mobility and supporting local economic development.

Thank you for your leadership in prioritizing safety and livability in our transportation infrastructure.

Sincerely,
David Hawkins & the following

s: Re: Active Safe and Slow Streets with Bikeway Projects Site
to: "Phillips; Taylor - DOTI CE0429 City Planner Senior" <taylor.phillips@denvergov.org>; "Thompson; Maggie
cc: Josh Mehlem <Josh.Mehlem@consoreng.com>; John Piccirillo <John.Piccirillo@consoreng.com>
b:
Hi Taylor, Maggie, & Molly,


I hope you're having a good week! We've added the below content into the newsletter format in Constant Contact (PDF attached) for your review. Let me know if you have any questions!


Best,
Emma







Emma Knisley
Planner
o: +1.720.901.3067

  ________________________________

From: Emma Knisley <emma.knisley@consoreng.com>
Sent: Tuesday, August 12, 2025 1:06 PM
To: Phillips, Taylor - DOTI CE0429 City Planner Senior <taylor.phillips@denvergov.org>
Cc: Josh Mehlem <Josh.Mehlem@consoreng.com>; John Piccirillo <John.Piccirillo@consoreng.com>
Subject: Re: Active Safe and Slow Streets with Bikeway Projects Site Updates


Hey Taylor,


Apologies for the delay on the SC corridors - please see the attached maps and descriptions below and let us know if you have any edits before they go on the website.


N Tennyson Street / W 7th Avenue: W 11th Avenue to W 7th Avenue to N Perry Street
Neighborhood(s): Villa Park
Project Description: This neighborhood bikeway will include pedestrian safety improvements such as curb extensions and pedestrian refuge islands to shorten crossing distances, traffic calming using speed humps to slow down vehicle speeds, and wayfinding signage. This project also features a climbing bike lane between 9th and 10th Avenues due to the steeper grade of this segment.
Project Status: Construction expected to begin in September 2025
Program: West Denver Safer Streets


E Virginia Ave / E Dakota Ave: S Broadway St to S Marion St Pkwy
Neighborhood(s): West Washington Park
Project Description: This project will include a buffered bike lane from Broadway to Lincoln St and a bike lane from Lincoln St to S Sherman St.  Additionally, the project includes a neighborhood bikeway on S Sherman St and E Dakota Ave, which includes pedestrian safety improvements like curb extensions as well as flashing lights to make it easier for people walking to cross busy streets along with traffic calming elements that reduce vehicle speeds such as traffic circles. Diverters will be installed at the intersections of E Virgina Ave & S Sherman St and E Dakota Ave & S Logan St to reduce cut-through traffic.
Project Status: Construction expected to begin in September 2025
Program: South Central Denver CTN



N Pearl St / N Emerson St: E 3rd Avenue to E 20th Avenue
Neighborhood(s): Speer, Capitol Hill, North Capitol Hill
Project Description: This neighborhood bikeway will include pedestrian safety improvements such as curb extensions and pedestrian refuge islands to shorten crossing distances, as well as flashing lights to make it easier for people walking to cross busy streets. The project will also include traffic calming measures that will slow down vehicle speeds such as speed humps and traffic circles, and bicycle intersection crossing improvements such as bike boxes on N Pearl St at the intersections of 13th Ave, 14th Ave, and 16th Ave.
Project Status: Construction expected to begin in August 2025
Program: South Central Denver CTN


Best,
Emma

to: Lanphier, Molly - DOTI Project Manager Engineering Senior <Molly.Lanphier@denvergov.org>; Cieciek,
b:
Klaus D & Marie K Heidrich
480 S Marion Pkwy
Denver, CO. 80209




August 27, 2025




Ms Molly Lanphier
Molly.Lanphier@Denvergov.org


Dear Ms Lanphier, It has come to our attention that major changes are planned for Alameda Av. between S. Franklin and S. Grant.  We live on the intersection of Marion Parkway and Virginia, so this project is right in “our backyard”.


We have lived here for going on 13 years and have seen the traffic growth on Alameda.  While we agree that adding a dedicated left turn lane is a good idea but to accomplish this by losing one entire lane of traffic is the wrong answer.  Yes, there currently is a very infrequent occasional backup in the left lane if a car is trying to turn left, crossing the opposing traffic.  Forcing all traffic, especially the morning and evening rush hour into one lane will cause major backups in this one remaining through lane for the entire length of the planned project.


Since there are no four lane East West through streets in this area, it will force frustrated drivers to available side streets.  The most desirable will become Virginia, a relatively quiet two lane street right by Wash Park, which definitely is not prepared to serve as an Alameda overflow.


We implore you to ask DOTI staff to rethink this drastic solution for a very minor problem.


Best regards,


Dieter and Marie

Dear Molly Lanphier
DOTI Liaison for Council DIstrict 6


ActforAlameda’s Key Concerns lists many issues that truly need to be addressed before this poorly planned lane repurposing project takes place, hopefully never.


The following are some added thoughts from a Wash Park East resident who lives in the Park Lane Condominiums.


It will be somewhat similar to Downing south from I25 to Evans but narrower & much more intrusive to the neighborhood.


No new sidewalks or curbs.


No recent traffic studies that include the redevelopment of the old Bed, Bath & Beyond site at University & 1st Av with possible 1000 apartments & new businesses.


No discussion of the need for 4way stop signs at EACH intersection in the neighborhoods that will see increased traffic as cars back up in the 1 lane each way on Alameda & divert to smaller neighborhood side streets.


Could find no discussion of additional safety for Steele School, the block Alameda to Dakota, Marion Pkwy to Lafayette.


Marion Pkwy is now a raceway for bicycles, motorcycles, construction trucks, cars, garbage trucks. Fire trucks are the only safe vehicles using Marion Pkwy.


Virginia Av & Dakota Av are narrow 2way 1 lane in each direction streets with parking on both sides that cannot handle the additional traffic that will avoid Alameda.


Additional noise & pollution in a premier, classic Denver neighborhood.


Cynthia Massirio
460 S Marion Pkwy 503C
80209
303 437-0492

Hey Taylor,

Apologies for the delay on the SC corridors - please see the attached maps and descriptions below and let us know if you have any edits before they go on the website.

N Tennyson Street / W 7th Avenue: W 11th Avenue to W 7th Avenue to N Perry Street
Neighborhood(s): Villa Park
Project Description: This neighborhood bikeway will include pedestrian safety improvements such as curb extensions and pedestrian refuge islands to shorten crossing distances, traffic calming using speed humps to slow down vehicle speeds, and wayfinding signage. This project also features a climbing bike lane between 9th and 10th Avenues due to the steeper grade of this segment.
Project Status: Construction expected to begin in September 2025
Program: West Denver Safer Streets

E Virginia Ave / E Dakota Ave: S Broadway St to S Marion St Pkwy
Neighborhood(s): West Washington Park
Project Description: This project will include a buffered bike lane from Broadway to Lincoln St and a bike lane from Lincoln St to S Sherman St.  Additionally, the project includes a neighborhood bikeway on S Sherman St and E Dakota Ave, which includes pedestrian safety improvements like curb extensions as well as flashing lights to make it easier for people walking to cross busy streets along with traffic calming elements that reduce vehicle speeds such as traffic circles. Diverters will be installed at the intersections of E Virgina Ave & S Sherman St and E Dakota Ave & S Logan St to reduce cut-through traffic.
Project Status: Construction expected to begin in September 2025
Program: South Central Denver CTN

N Pearl St / N Emerson St: E 3rd Avenue to E 20th Avenue
Neighborhood(s): Speer, Capitol Hill, North Capitol Hill
Project Description: This neighborhood bikeway will include pedestrian safety improvements such as curb extensions and pedestrian refuge islands to shorten crossing distances, as well as flashing lights to make it easier for people walking to cross busy streets. The project will also include traffic calming measures that will slow down vehicle speeds such as speed humps and traffic circles, and bicycle intersection crossing improvements such as bike boxes on N Pearl St at the intersections of 13th Ave, 14th Ave, and 16th Ave.
Project Status: Construction expected to begin in August 2025
Program: South Central Denver CTN

Best,
Emma

Dear Amy Ford,

DOTI Executive Director



ActforAlameda’s Key Concerns lists many issues that truly need to be addressed before this poorly planned lane repurposing project takes place, hopefully never.



The following are some added thoughts from a Wash Park East resident who lives in the Park Lane Condominiums.



It will be somewhat similar to Downing south from I25 to Evans but narrower & much more intrusive to the neighborhood.



No new sidewalks or curbs.



No recent traffic studies that include the redevelopment of the old Bed, Bath & Beyond site at University & 1st Av with possible 1000 apartments & new businesses.



No discussion of the need for 4way stop signs at EACH intersection in the neighborhoods that will see increased traffic as cars back up in the 1 lane each way on Alameda & divert to smaller neighborhood side streets.



Could find no discussion of additional safety for Steele School, the block Alameda to Dakota, Marion Pkwy to Lafayette.



Marion Pkwy is now a raceway for bicycles, motorcycles, construction trucks, cars, garbage trucks. Fire trucks are the only safe vehicles using Marion Pkwy.



Virginia Av & Dakota Av are narrow 2way 1 lane in each direction streets with parking on both sides that cannot handle the additional traffic that will avoid Alameda.



Additional noise & pollution in a premier, classic Denver neighborhood.



Cynthia Massirio

460 S Marion Pkwy 503C

80209

303 437-0492

From: Nancy Picucci <nlantis@gmail.com>
Sent: Thursday, August 28, 2025 4:25:16 PM
To: Ford, Amy - DOTI HE3258 Manager Department of Transportation and Infrastructure <Amy.Ford@denvergov.org>
Subject: [EXTERNAL] Alameda Project
Good day-

I have been thrilled to hear that Alameda Lane Repurposing Project was a GO. That street in that 12 block stretch has a lot of pedestrian traffic and many children that must cross it to attend nearby public schools and walk to friend’s homes. A reduced speed limit is key. While I am not usually a proponent of bike lanes, I think this plan makes sense due to the children in the neighborhood getting themselves to and from school and the number of people in the community that commute to and from work via bikes. People currently drive 45+ mph down Alameda and Downing when possible. During school hours people shoot through stop lights and stop signs endangering pedestrians. Reducing the lanes to 2 will increase visibility for cars and pedestrians. Too many people have been injured and died already. Please work to continue the Alameda Project to keep our communities safe and increasing the walkability of our neighborhood.

Many thanks,
Nancy Picucci
303.356.0598

Hello,
 I am writing in support of the proposed Alameda Ave changes to slow traffic and make it more safe for pedestrians. I was around for the last debacle and know the success of this plan is not guaranteed but, people are driving faster these days in heavier vehicles, particularly pickup trucks. The police seem to have vanished so people feel free to speed and weave.

This process has been ongoing for years and years. There have been numerous notices that were seen by neighbors who take the time to read.

Alameda has been a struggle for the city and state for decades, especially since 1936 when a flood along Cherry Creek took out the Alameda bridge crossing the creek that obviously was never rebuilt. Perhaps it wasn't due to it being during the Great Depression but, given my experience working with neighborhoods, I suspect that the wealthier residents, such as the earlier Polo Club members, have been very glad, and vocal, against changes that are perceived as less attractive for them, even tho this is a state road and wasn't designed as a residential street.

Let's hope this plan works. Something must be done.

Sarah O. McCarthy
c: 303.204.8722

Thanks for the help as I get up to speed -



steven

"Yes, we are in the process of working with our consultant to scope the new hybrid design. As part of the effort, we plan to update parts of the traffic analysis so that we can answer questions about diversion, queuing, and delay. "

Let's try to focus on these three asks during scoping to keep budget under control. Thanks.

steven

Hi Matt,

Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.

*	
	Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm (following your current design)
*	
	Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound movements. Additionally, we would like the design to include upgraded controller cabinet  and ADA compliant ramps (reconstruction of all elements on that corner).


Please include the Emerson items as a standalone scope item as we determine funding for the improvements. Thanks again,

steven

City & County of Denver

Phone: 720.437.0022

From: Ciarkowski, Matt <matt.ciarkowski@kimley-horn.com>
Sent: Tuesday, September 16, 2025 11:02 AM
To: Marfitano, Steven C. - DOTI CE0397 Engineer <Steven.Marfitano@denvergov.org>
Cc: Boncore, Brett - DOTI CE0403 Engineer-Architect Supervisor <Brett.Boncore@denvergov.org>; Pratt, Anthony <Anthony.Pratt@kimley-horn.com>; Gormley, Mary <Mary.Gormley@kimley-horn.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL] RE: ALR Coordination 
Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.

 

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

*	Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.
*	Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.
*	Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.
*	Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.
*	Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.
*	Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

 

Matt Ciarkowski, P.E. (CO, TX, WA, WY, NE) 
Kimley-Horn | 1125 17th Street, Suite 1400, Denver, CO 80202
Direct: 720.943.5660 | Mobile: 817.308.7306

Cc: Boncore, Brett - DOTI CE0403 Engineer-Architect Supervisor <Brett.Boncore@denvergov.org>
Subject: Re: ALR Coordination

 

Hi Matt, 

 

I wanted to follow up about the traffic signal modification request from my commented SOW. I did a review yesterday to identify the signalized intersection with permitted left turns in the eastbound direction along Alameda. Our main concern is that the signal mast arm may not be long enough to properly align the signal head above the lane. I identified only one location of major concern - Emerson. I am hoping you and your team can review this assumption, and also do your own exploration to determine where we might need to modify the signal heads to properly control movements in the eastbound and westbound directions. 

 

I do have a call tomorrow morning and am hoping you can provide me the draft scope and fee proposal by 9am tomorrow morning. Let me know if that won't be possible - thanks again.

 

steven

Subject: [EXTERNAL] RE: ALR Coordination 
Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.

 

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

*	Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.
*	Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.
*	Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.
*	Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.
*	Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.
*	Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

 

Matt Ciarkowski, P.E. (CO, TX, WA, WY, NE)

RE: ALR Coordination Ciarkowski, Matt matt.ciarkowski@kimley-horn.com "Marfitano; Steven C. - DOTI CE0397 Engineer" <Steven.Marfitano@denvergov.org> "Boncore; Brett - DOTI CE0403 Engineer-Architect Supervisor" <Brett.Boncore@denvergov.org>; "Pratt; Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our
correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still
works for you.
The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below
regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the
design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired
approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.
* Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary. * Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to
align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road. * Washington – Minor
signal equipment adjustments may be necessary. * Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align
with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road. * Downing – Minor signal
equipment adjustments may be necessary. * Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.
I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and
recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor
equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping
perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case
scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm
modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.
Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the
exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi Matt,
I wanted to follow up about the traffic signal modification request from my commented SOW. I did a review
yesterday to identify the signalized intersection with permitted left turns in the eastbound direction along
Alameda. Our main concern is that the signal mast arm may not be long enough to properly align the signal
head above the lane. I identified only one location of major concern - Emerson. I am hoping you and your team
can review this assumption, and also do your own exploration to determine where we might need to modify the
signal heads to properly control movements in the eastbound and westbound directions.
I do have a call tomorrow morning and am hoping you can provide me the draft scope and fee proposal by 9am
tomorrow morning. Let me know if that won't be possible - thanks again.
steven

Hi Matt, 

I wanted to follow up about the traffic signal modification request from my commented SOW. I did a review yesterday to identify the signalized intersection with permitted left turns in the eastbound direction along Alameda. Our main concern is that the signal mast arm may not be long enough to properly align the signal head above the lane. I identified only one location of major concern - Emerson. I am hoping you and your team can review this assumption, and also do your own exploration to determine where we might need to modify the signal heads to properly control movements in the eastbound and westbound directions. 

I do have a call tomorrow morning and am hoping you can provide me the draft scope and fee proposal by 9am tomorrow morning. Let me know if that won't be possible - thanks again.

steven

cc: "Boncore; Brett - DOTI CE0403 Engineer-Architect Supervisor" <Brett.Boncore@denvergov.org>; "Pratt;
b:
Hi Matt,

I wanted to follow up about the traffic signal modification request from my commented SOW. I did a review yesterday to identify the signalized intersection with permitted left turns in the eastbound direction along Alameda. Our main concern is that the signal mast arm may not be long enough to properly align the signal head above the lane. I identified only one location of major concern - Emerson. I am hoping you and your team can review this assumption, and also do your own exploration to determine where we might need to modify the signal heads to properly control movements in the eastbound and westbound directions.

I do have a call tomorrow morning and am hoping you can provide me the draft scope and fee proposal by 9am tomorrow morning. Let me know if that won't be possible - thanks again.

steven

steven


Steven Marfitano, PE | Engineer
City & County of Denver
Department of Transportation & Infrastructure | Transportation Implementation Division

Phone: 720.437.0022

steven.marfitano@denvergov.org<mailto:steven.marfitano@denvergov.org>

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

*         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

*         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

*         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

*         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

*         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

*         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi Matt,
Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.
* Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced
after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm
(following your current design) <-- Bullet added to the Project Understanding section of the Agreement
confirming approach to signal improvements.
* Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound
movements. Additionally, we would like the design to include upgraded controller cabinet and ADA compliant
ramps (reconstruction of all elements on that corner). <-- I have added a very high level approach to this signal
design in Add Alternate Task 7. Due to the time constraints of this change, I won’t be able to provide a full
scope and fee at this time; but have rather noted this as “TBD”. I know this isn’t ideal; however, based on my
understanding of the overall situation from our call yesterday, it seems like this can be acceptable for your call
today. Please see below for a few considerations that will impact the scope/fee that will need to be confirmed
prior to finalizing.
* Interconnect – I am emailing Josh Jones to confirm what the existing interconnect is along the corridor, if any.
We will need to plan to connect this into the revised cabinet location if present. * Survey – I’ll need to get a
quote for this data collection at the intersection. * Power – It doesn’t appear that there is an existing pedestal
service at the intersection based on Google StreetView. I’ll need to swing by later to confirm. If this doesn’t
exist, the City typically requires that this be included with modification/construction. Would the City like us to
include scope for an electrical one-line diagram and coordination with the service provider (Xcel)? * Drainage –
There is an existing inlet on the SEC (Google StreetView <https://www.google.com/maps/@39.7110705,-104.9
769204,3a,75y,111.2h,90.97t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g!2e0!6shttps:*2F*2Fstreetvie

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.

 

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

*         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

*         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

*         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

*         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

*         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

*         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi Matt,



Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.

  *   Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm (following your current design) <-- Bullet added to the Project Understanding section of the Agreement confirming approach to signal improvements.

  *   Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound movements. Please see below for a few considerations that will impact the scope/fee that will need to be confirmed prior to finalizing.

     *   Interconnect – I am emailing Josh Jones to confirm what the existing interconnect is along the corridor, if any. We will need to plan to connect this into the revised cabinet location if present.
     *   Survey – I’ll need to get a quote for this data collection at the intersection.
     *   Power – It doesn’t appear that there is an existing pedestal service at the intersection based on Google StreetView. I’ll need to swing by later to confirm. If this doesn’t exist, the City typically requires that this be included with modification/construction. Would the City like us to include scope for an electrical one-line diagram and coordination with the service provider (Xcel)?
     *   Drainage – There is an existing inlet on the SEC (Google StreetView<https://www.google.com/maps/@39.7110705,-104.9769204,3a,75y,111.2h,90.97t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g!2e0!6shttps:*2F*2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com*2Fv1*2Fthumbnail*3Fcb_client*3Dmaps_sv.tactile*26w*3D900*26h*3D600*26pitch*3D-0.9708904989167593*26panoid*3DK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g*26yaw*3D111.19549616723357!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MDkxNC4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw*3D*3D>). Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.



The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

·         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

·         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

·         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

·         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

·         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

·         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.



I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.



Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.



The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

·         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

·         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

·         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

·         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

·         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

·         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.



I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.



Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.

 

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

Hi Matt,

 

Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.

*	Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm (following your current design) <-- Bullet added to the Project Understanding section of the Agreement confirming approach to signal improvements.

*	Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound movements. Additionally, we would like the design to include upgraded controller cabinet  and ADA compliant ramps (reconstruction of all elements on that corner). <-- I have added a very high level approach to this signal design in Add Alternate Task 7. Due to the time constraints of this change, I won’t be able to provide a full scope and fee at this time; but have rather noted this as “TBD”. I know this isn’t ideal; however, based on my understanding of the overall situation from our call yesterday, it seems like this can be acceptable for your call today. Please see below for a few considerations that will impact the scope/fee that will need to be confirmed prior to finalizing.

	*	Interconnect – I am emailing Josh Jones to confirm what the existing interconnect is along the corridor, if any. We will need to plan to connect this into the revised cabinet location if present.
	*	Survey – I’ll need to get a quote for this data collection at the intersection.
	*	Power – It doesn’t appear that there is an existing pedestal service at the intersection based on Google StreetView. I’ll need to swing by later to confirm. If this doesn’t exist, the City typically requires that this be included with modification/construction. Would the City like us to include scope for an electrical one-line diagram and coordination with the service provider (Xcel)?
	*	Drainage – There is an existing inlet on the SEC (Google StreetView <https://www.google.com/maps/@39.7110705,-104.9769204,3a,75y,111.2h,90.97t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g!2e0!6shttps:*2F*2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com*2Fv1*2Fthumbnail*3Fcb_client*3Dmaps_sv.tactile*26w*3D900*26h*3D600*26pitch*3D-0.9708904989167593*26panoid*3DK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g*26yaw*3D111.19549616723357!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MDkxNC4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw*3D*3D> ). Elsewhere along the corridor, existing inlets have been maintained with reconstruction activities around them. I’m assuming that we want to take the same approach at this intersection. We will plan to not include any drainage improvements or analysis.

 

Please include the Emerson items as a standalone scope item as we determine funding for the improvements. Thanks again,

 

steven

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.

 

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi Matt,

 

Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.

*	Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm (following your current design) <-- Bullet added to the Project Understanding section of the Agreement confirming approach to signal improvements.

*	Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound movements. Additionally, we would like the design to include upgraded controller cabinet  and ADA compliant ramps (reconstruction of all elements on that corner). <-- I have added a very high level approach to this signal design in Add Alternate Task 7. Due to the time constraints of this change, I won’t be able to provide a full scope and fee at this time; but have rather noted this as “TBD”. I know this isn’t ideal; however, based on my understanding of the overall situation from our call yesterday, it seems like this can be acceptable for your call today. Please see below for a few considerations that will impact the scope/fee that will need to be confirmed prior to finalizing.

	*	Interconnect – I am emailing Josh Jones to confirm what the existing interconnect is along the corridor, if any. We will need to plan to connect this into the revised cabinet location if present.
	*	Survey – I’ll need to get a quote for this data collection at the intersection.
	*	Power – It doesn’t appear that there is an existing pedestal service at the intersection based on Google StreetView. I’ll need to swing by later to confirm. If this doesn’t exist, the City typically requires that this be included with modification/construction. Would the City like us to include scope for an electrical one-line diagram and coordination with the service provider (Xcel)?
	*	Drainage – There is an existing inlet on the SEC (Google StreetView <https://www.google.com/maps/@39.7110705,-104.9769204,3a,75y,111.2h,90.97t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g!2e0!6shttps:*2F*2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com*2Fv1*2Fthumbnail*3Fcb_client*3Dmaps_sv.tactile*26w*3D900*26h*3D600*26pitch*3D-0.9708904989167593*26panoid*3DK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g*26yaw*3D111.19549616723357!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MDkxNC4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw*3D*3D> ). Elsewhere along the corridor, existing inlets have been maintained with reconstruction activities around them. I’m assuming that we want to take the same approach at this intersection. We will plan to not include any drainage improvements or analysis.

 

Please include the Emerson items as a standalone scope item as we determine funding for the improvements. Thanks again,

 

steven

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.

 

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

 

I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.

 

Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

They can’t change their rates, but they can use any of the rates/classifications from the attached contract. So they could change the classification if need be for the Task Order Change as long as it aligns with the attached contract. Make sense?

b:
Hi Matt, thanks for the time to connect - here's the analysis I had started - please focus on the bulleted changes (not on the fee, which are obviously just my assumptions). Def call or write if there's any questions. I will let you know how my conversation with Brett goes on Monday.

Here was the original fee:
[cid:18e639ad-8b83-4984-85f4-50a632602cfb]
The original schedule lasted 9 months with the first 3-4 months representing the data collection and analysis.

The goal here is to identify the essential deliverables for a MVP; which means preserving the design for Alameda, signal timings for the corridor, and PI support. Here are the scope items we will need to cut to get towards $50k.

  1.
Remove all data collection and majority traffic safety analysis and evaluation
This includes removal of all traffic counts, delay analysis, diversion analysis, KHA review and validation of the safety assessment, and memo/documentation. The only item preserved from this task would be development of future signal timings, and field observation and verification of timing upon implementation. This will mean we cannot answer queuing specific questions from Amy Ford's email on 9/3.
(these changes should reduce the schedule by approx 3 months, reducing task 1 by 33%; eliminate all data collection; and reduce traffic eval by 80%).

  2.
In order to be able to answer questions about diversion, I have already talked to Rinal about using the travel demand model to make some measure of this by using the DRCOG travel demand model. I will need support from the team to prioritize this effort so that we can give a high-level diversion answer to the public. We will not incorporate this information into further operational analysis such as queuing. (If this is not preferred/possible, we could make a very crude assumption that because only half the corridor capacity reduction is planned only those volumes would be impacted - traffic counts show approximately 45% of traffic moves WB so we could assume 4.5% diversion. This is not preferred, because congestion induced diversion is not linear with volume and this would likely over-estimate our actual diversion impacts.)


  1.
Scaled back public engagement support - given the removal of the traffic evaluation, materials updated provided by KHA will include translation and materials demonstrating the project components. We will use the materials prepared for the Aug Amy Ford meeting as a basis for the public information and may ask KHA to provide graphical support. (assume 60% reduction)

  2.
Transfer Downing/Virginia In-house - many of the alternatives identified with Eric are easily accomplished in house with TME support and implemented by maintenance. Further discussion is needed to determine which components might be necessarily done by consultant and if the installation would be maintenance or construction. (assumes $9k design cost reduction (17%))


  1.
Preserve all signal modification design as these are essential to the safety performance of the corridor. May explore only signal pole replacement at Emerson by Ops, but design would be provided by KHA in case construction dollars can be found for the full pole, cabinet, and sidewalk work. At Pearl, would include bolted-on mast arm replacement knowing that if construction dollars are not available, the existing mast arm could be used.

  2.
Removal of one review stage. Given the simplification of the overall scope of work (initially included 60%, 90%, 100%, and Last Call) and the fact this plan set has already been all the way through our review process; propose removal of the 60% plan set and review.  (assumes $9k design cost reduction (17%)) [Matt - this was my thinking before you proposed a redlined plan set, which I think we should continue to explore so we preserve budget capacity for Task 7.)

Hi Matt,



Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.

  *   Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm (following your current design) <-- Bullet added to the Project Understanding section of the Agreement confirming approach to signal improvements.
  *   Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound movements. Additionally, we would like the design to include upgraded controller cabinet  and ADA compliant ramps (reconstruction of all elements on that corner). <-- I have added a very high level approach to this signal design in Add Alternate Task 7. Due to the time constraints of this change, I won’t be able to provide a full scope and fee at this time; but have rather noted this as “TBD”. I know this isn’t ideal; however, based on my understanding of the overall situation from our call yesterday, it seems like this can be acceptable for your call today. Please see below for a few considerations that will impact the scope/fee that will need to be confirmed prior to finalizing.
     *   Interconnect – I am emailing Josh Jones to confirm what the existing interconnect is along the corridor, if any. We will need to plan to connect this into the revised cabinet location if present.
     *   Survey – I’ll need to get a quote for this data collection at the intersection.
     *   Power – It doesn’t appear that there is an existing pedestal service at the intersection based on Google StreetView. I’ll need to swing by later to confirm. If this doesn’t exist, the City typically requires that this be included with modification/construction. Would the City like us to include scope for an electrical one-line diagram and coordination with the service provider (Xcel)?
     *   Drainage – There is an existing inlet on the SEC (Google StreetView<https://www.google.com/maps/@39.7110705,-104.9769204,3a,75y,111.2h,90.97t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g!2e0!6shttps:*2F*2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com*2Fv1*2Fthumbnail*3Fcb_client*3Dmaps_sv.tactile*26w*3D900*26h*3D600*26pitch*3D-0.9708904989167593*26panoid*3DK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g*26yaw*3D111.19549616723357!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MDkxNC4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw*3D*3D>). Elsewhere along the corridor, existing inlets have been maintained with reconstruction activities around them. I’m assuming that we want to take the same approach at this intersection. We will plan to not include any drainage improvements or analysis.



Please include the Emerson items as a standalone scope item as we determine funding for the improvements. Thanks again,

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.



The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

•         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

•         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

•         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

•         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

•         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

•         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.



I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.



Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks



Matt Ciarkowski, P.E. (CO, TX, WA, WY, NE)

Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

Hi Matt, thanks for the time to connect - here's the analysis I had started - please focus on the bulleted changes (not on the fee, which are obviously just my assumptions). Def call or write if there's any questions. I will let you know how my conversation with Brett goes on Monday.



Here was the original fee:

[cid:image003.png@01DC2BE2.6BA0B610]

The original schedule lasted 9 months with the first 3-4 months representing the data collection and analysis.



The goal here is to identify the essential deliverables for a MVP; which means preserving the design for Alameda, signal timings for the corridor, and PI support. Here are the scope items we will need to cut to get towards $50k.

  1.  Remove all data collection and majority traffic safety analysis and evaluation

This includes removal of all traffic counts, delay analysis, diversion analysis, KHA review and validation of the safety assessment, and memo/documentation. The only item preserved from this task would be development of future signal timings, and field observation and verification of timing upon implementation. This will mean we cannot answer queuing specific questions from Amy Ford's email on 9/3.

(these changes should reduce the schedule by approx 3 months, reducing task 1 by 33%; eliminate all data collection; and reduce traffic eval by 80%).



  1.  In order to be able to answer questions about diversion, I have already talked to Rinal about using the travel demand model to make some measure of this by using the DRCOG travel demand model. I will need support from the team to prioritize this effort so that we can give a high-level diversion answer to the public. We will not incorporate this information into further operational analysis such as queuing. (If this is not preferred/possible, we could make a very crude assumption that because only half the corridor capacity reduction is planned only those volumes would be impacted - traffic counts show approximately 45% of traffic moves WB so we could assume 4.5% diversion. This is not preferred, because congestion induced diversion is not linear with volume and this would likely over-estimate our actual diversion impacts.)



  1.  Scaled back public engagement support - given the removal of the traffic evaluation, materials updated provided by KHA will include translation and materials demonstrating the project components. We will use the materials prepared for the Aug Amy Ford meeting as a basis for the public information and may ask KHA to provide graphical support. (assume 60% reduction)



  1.  Transfer Downing/Virginia In-house - many of the alternatives identified with Eric are easily accomplished in house with TME support and implemented by maintenance. Further discussion is needed to determine which components might be necessarily done by consultant and if the installation would be maintenance or construction. (assumes $9k design cost reduction (17%))



  1.  Preserve all signal modification design as these are essential to the safety performance of the corridor. May explore only signal pole replacement at Emerson by Ops, but design would be provided by KHA in case construction dollars can be found for the full pole, cabinet, and sidewalk work. At Pearl, would include bolted-on mast arm replacement knowing that if construction dollars are not available, the existing mast arm could be used.



  1.  Removal of one review stage. Given the simplification of the overall scope of work (initially included 60%, 90%, 100%, and Last Call) and the fact this plan set has already been all the way through our review process; propose removal of the 60% plan set and review.  (assumes $9k design cost reduction (17%)) [Matt - this was my thinking before you proposed a redlined plan set, which I think we should continue to explore so we preserve budget capacity for Task 7.)

The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

•         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

•         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

•         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

•         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

•         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

•         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.



I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.



Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks

Hi Matt, thanks for the time to connect - here's the analysis I had started - please focus on the bulleted changes (not on the fee, which are obviously just my assumptions). Def call or write if there's any questions. I will let you know how my conversation with Brett goes on Monday.

Here was the original fee:
[cid:image003.png@01DC2BE2.6BA0B610]
The original schedule lasted 9 months with the first 3-4 months representing the data collection and analysis.

The goal here is to identify the essential deliverables for a MVP; which means preserving the design for Alameda, signal timings for the corridor, and PI support. Here are the scope items we will need to cut to get towards $50k.

  1.  Remove all data collection and majority traffic safety analysis and evaluation
This includes removal of all traffic counts, delay analysis, diversion analysis, KHA review and validation of the safety assessment, and memo/documentation. The only item preserved from this task would be development of future signal timings, and field observation and verification of timing upon implementation. This will mean we cannot answer queuing specific questions from Amy Ford's email on 9/3.
(these changes should reduce the schedule by approx 3 months, reducing task 1 by 33%; eliminate all data collection; and reduce traffic eval by 80%).


  1.  In order to be able to answer questions about diversion, I have already talked to Rinal about using the travel demand model to make some measure of this by using the DRCOG travel demand model. I will need support from the team to prioritize this effort so that we can give a high-level diversion answer to the public. We will not incorporate this information into further operational analysis such as queuing. (If this is not preferred/possible, we could make a very crude assumption that because only half the corridor capacity reduction is planned only those volumes would be impacted - traffic counts show approximately 45% of traffic moves WB so we could assume 4.5% diversion. This is not preferred, because congestion induced diversion is not linear with volume and this would likely over-estimate our actual diversion impacts.)


  1.  Scaled back public engagement support - given the removal of the traffic evaluation, materials updated provided by KHA will include translation and materials demonstrating the project components. We will use the materials prepared for the Aug Amy Ford meeting as a basis for the public information and may ask KHA to provide graphical support. (assume 60% reduction)


  1.  Transfer Downing/Virginia In-house - many of the alternatives identified with Eric are easily accomplished in house with TME support and implemented by maintenance. Further discussion is needed to determine which components might be necessarily done by consultant and if the installation would be maintenance or construction. (assumes $9k design cost reduction (17%))


  1.  Preserve all signal modification design as these are essential to the safety performance of the corridor. May explore only signal pole replacement at Emerson by Ops, but design would be provided by KHA in case construction dollars can be found for the full pole, cabinet, and sidewalk work. At Pearl, would include bolted-on mast arm replacement knowing that if construction dollars are not available, the existing mast arm could be used.


  1.  Removal of one review stage. Given the simplification of the overall scope of work (initially included 60%, 90%, 100%, and Last Call) and the fact this plan set has already been all the way through our review process; propose removal of the 60% plan set and review.  (assumes $9k design cost reduction (17%)) [Matt - this was my thinking before you proposed a redlined plan set, which I think we should continue to explore so we preserve budget capacity for Task 7.)

Hi Matt,



Thanks for the time this afternoon, here are a couple more answers as you refine the scope/fee.

  *   Pearl - please assume that the existing signal pole will be maintained and either a) the arm will be replaced after confirmation that the footing/design is adequate or b) the left turn head will be added onto the existing arm (following your current design) <-- Bullet added to the Project Understanding section of the Agreement confirming approach to signal improvements.
  *   Emerson - I have confirmed that we would like to include replacement of the signal pole for the eastbound movements. Additionally, we would like the design to include upgraded controller cabinet  and ADA compliant ramps (reconstruction of all elements on that corner). <-- I have added a very high level approach to this signal design in Add Alternate Task 7. Due to the time constraints of this change, I won’t be able to provide a full scope and fee at this time; but have rather noted this as “TBD”. I know this isn’t ideal; however, based on my understanding of the overall situation from our call yesterday, it seems like this can be acceptable for your call today. Please see below for a few considerations that will impact the scope/fee that will need to be confirmed prior to finalizing.
     *   Interconnect – I am emailing Josh Jones to confirm what the existing interconnect is along the corridor, if any. We will need to plan to connect this into the revised cabinet location if present.
     *   Survey – I’ll need to get a quote for this data collection at the intersection.
     *   Power – It doesn’t appear that there is an existing pedestal service at the intersection based on Google StreetView. I’ll need to swing by later to confirm. If this doesn’t exist, the City typically requires that this be included with modification/construction. Would the City like us to include scope for an electrical one-line diagram and coordination with the service provider (Xcel)?
     *   Drainage – There is an existing inlet on the SEC (Google StreetView<https://www.google.com/maps/@39.7110705,-104.9769204,3a,75y,111.2h,90.97t/data=!3m7!1e1!3m5!1sK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g!2e0!6shttps:*2F*2Fstreetviewpixels-pa.googleapis.com*2Fv1*2Fthumbnail*3Fcb_client*3Dmaps_sv.tactile*26w*3D900*26h*3D600*26pitch*3D-0.9708904989167593*26panoid*3DK0Lx2RV39Y8mrNn_hkpE4g*26yaw*3D111.19549616723357!7i16384!8i8192?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI1MDkxNC4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw*3D*3D>). Elsewhere along the corridor, existing inlets have been maintained with reconstruction activities around them. I’m assuming that we want to take the same approach at this intersection. We will plan to not include any drainage improvements or analysis.



Please include the Emerson items as a standalone scope item as we determine funding for the improvements. Thanks again,

Hi, Steven. Thanks for the email. I’m realizing now that I never sent the invitation for us to connect following our correspondence about a meeting time this week, my apologies. I’ll send now for 2P, assuming that time still works for you.



The signal modifications are the largest outstanding item in our approach. Please see a few notes below regarding an initial review of the intersections. I’d like to confirm the City’s desired approach for the design/construction of modified signal structures on these corners or if signal head modification is the desired approach. This will obviously impact the design and construction costs.

•         Logan – Signal modification/reconstruction not necessary.

•         Pearl – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

•         Washington – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

•         Emerson – Mast arm extension on SEC is needed to align with center of future EBLT. Arm currently only extends to the center of the road.

•         Downing – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.

•         Marion – Minor signal equipment adjustments may be necessary.



I was also hoping to discuss the Virginia & Downing intersection with you in more detail. Pending analysis and recommendation, there may be signal modifications at this intersection, but they are likely limited to minor equipment modifications. More importantly, you mentioned the potential of curb extensions. From a scoping perspective, I’m trying to determine if we should budget for this condition which would be our worst-case scenario level of effort. Furthermore, we would need to consider effort for design survey and storm modifications. I’d like to get your input on how to approach at this stage of the project.



Regarding the status of a draft agreement; we are on track to have a draft developed by EOD with the exception of the items noted above. Let’s connect later to discuss these items. Thanks
